description: Voice actor and studio reputation via Wikipedia2Vec (300-dim). Quantifies production 'star power' but is noisy alone — many actors and studios are unknown entities with sparse graph connections.
Synopsis text embeddings via JINA-CLIP-v2. Performance is limited by English-centric pretraining on Japanese text, and inconsistent synopsis styles (some are promotional, others descriptive).

Poster visual features extracted via CLIP (pretrained on 400M image-text pairs). Captures art style, color palette, and character design. Studios invest heavily in key visuals — posters often generate pre-broadcast buzz.
Release year, studio, genre tags, and broadcast slot. The strongest single modality — captures temporal market cycles, genre supply/demand, and studio track records.
Metadata + Image. Powerful combination: temporal and genre context (M) paired with visual identity signal (I). Cast/Production adds a modest but consistent gain on top of this strong baseline.

All four modalities combined. Adding Text (T) HURTS performance compared to M+I+C — noisy Japanese synopsis embeddings introduce irrelevant variance that dilutes signal from the stronger modalities. More features ≠ better performance.
BEST COMBINATION — AP 0.6627. Metadata + Image + Cast/Production. The production network confirms whether the team behind the anime has the track record to execute successfully.
